rows.description: Accrued interest expenses, interest payable in commercial banks.
Accounts receivable included in current assets for the annual period.
aggregate of accumulated depreciation for each fixed asset.
The fiscal month number corresponding to the end of the annual period.
The fiscal year associated with the annual reporting period.
Net income reported for the annual period.
The currency code in which annual financial values are presented after conversion.
total assets of the company.
Total current assets. Sum of cash and short-term investments, total receivables (net), total inventory, prepaid expenses, other current assets.
Other Current Assets, Total.
Tangible Book Value per Share, Common Equity.
capital expenditures.
Total current assets - total net receivables.
cash and short-term investments.
cash from financing activities that is the sum of financing cash flow items, cash dividends paid, issuance of stock, net and issuance of debt, net.
Cash from Investing Activities.
Includes diverse cash flows that are reported outside of the operating, investing, and financing sections of the cash flow statement.
cost of revenue represents total costs that can be directly attributed to the goods and services produced/purchased and sold.
Cost of revenue including labor costs reported within cost of goods sold & that are not clearly labeled as cost of goods sold.
Currency in which the financial values have been converted for reporting.
Total current assets/total current liabilities.
Total long-term plus short-term debt.
Represents interest-bearing debt with maturities beyond 1 year. Long-term debt may consist of long-term bank borrowings, bonds, convertible bonds, etc.
Current Port. of LT Debt/Capital Leases.
Notes payable/short-term debt
Depreciation of fixed assets reported outside of the cost of goods and services sold.
depreciation.
Normalized Earnings before Interest and Taxes.
Normalized Earnings before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation and Amortization

represents the bottom-line earnings per share available to common stockholders, including the effects of extraordinary items - adjusted by minority interest, equity in affiliates, the U.S. GAAP adjustment, preferred distributions and all other adjustments to earnings per share.
total equity.
Long-Term Debt Maturing within 1 Year
Capital Lease Payments Due in Year 1
Operating Lease Payments Due in Year 1
Treasury Shares - Common Stock Primary Issue

Represents the number of common shares outstanding in a company. When a company has 2 or more different types of common shares outstanding, Shares Outstanding - Common Stock Primary Issue represents the primary issue, which is most actively traded in the market.
common dividends per share for common stock primary issues.
Standardized Long-Term Debt Maturing in Year 5
Operating Lease Payments Due in Year 2
Standardized Long-Term Debt Maturing in Years 2 and 3 represents the summation of Standardized Long-Term Debt Maturing in Year 2 and Standardized Long-Term Debt Maturing in Year 3.
Standardized Operating Lease Payments Due in Years 2 and 3 represents the summation of Standardized Operating Lease Payments Due in Year 2 and Standardized Operating Lease Payments Due in Year 3.
Long Term Debt Maturing in Year 3
Standardized Operating Lease Payments Due in Year 3.
Long Term Debt Maturing in Year 4
Standardized Long Term Debt Maturing in Years 4 and 5
Standardized long-term debt maturing in year 5
other current assets.
Other Current Assets, Total.
Total current assets. Sum of cash and short-term investments, total receivables (net), total inventory, prepaid expenses, other current assets.
Normalized Earnings before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation and Amortization

represents provisions established for doubtful accounts receivable. Provision for doubtful accounts is usually established on the basis of maturities of receivables and the company's experience on collecting such receivables.
Sale of assets to be leased out under operating lease contracts.
Selling/General/Administrative Expense.
Selling/General/Administrative Expenses, Total.
Accumulated Intangible Amortization represents accumulated amortization against intangible fixed assets.
Count of unique IDs of industry participants. Industry stands for an aggregate view of all  equity clearance activity for the date, symbol, and transaction type in question.

Equity in Affiliates, Supplemental represents the total share of earnings/losses, net of taxes, that the company is entitled to from unconsolidated associated companies, affiliates or joint ventures, which have not been distributed as dividends.
loss/gain on sale of assets listed in the operating section.
Gain (Loss) on Sale of Assets.
Other Long-Term Assets, Total.
Amortization of Acquisition Costs, Supplemental
annual financial values.
Total Plan Assets

represents translation gains (losses) on financial statements of foreign subsidiaries. Such adjustments may be required when the currency of a subsidiary is different from the reporting currency of the reporting company.
deferred income taxes - long term. These are credits being applied to income taxes that will be paid in future years.
Standardized Long Term Debt Maturing in Year 6 & Beyond

Standardized Operating Lease Payments Due in Year 6 and Beyond represents Total Operating Leases, Supplemental less Standardized Operating Lease Payments Due within 1 Year, Standardized Operating Lease Payments Due in Year 2, Standardized Operating Lease Payments Due in Year 3, Standardized Operating Lease Payments Due in Year 4 and Standardized Operating Lease Payments Due in Year 5.
Net Cash - Beginning Balance represents the beginning balance of cash and cash equivalents, as defined by a company.
other liabilities
Deconsolidation of a subsidiary classified as Sale of Business.
Accrued interest expenses, interest payable in commercial banks
Represents changes in accounts payable during the period. An increase in accounts payable has a positive impact on cash flow from operating activities, and vice versa.
Dividends Payable
Other Payables
Income Available to Common Excl. Extraordinary Items

Normalized Income Available to Common represents the bottom-line net income available to common shareholders, excluding the effects of all non-recurring/unusual/one-off/extraordinary items, after minority interest, equity in affiliates, the U.S. GAAP adjustment, preferred distributions, and all other adjustments to net income.
represents the gross amount of buildings and their attachments, before being reduced by accumulated depreciation of the buildings.
Net Change in Cash
Deferred Revenue - Current

Represents the acquisition cost of machinery and other equipment (including delivery equipment, office equipment, machinery, furniture and fixtures, furnishings, factory equipment, tools, etc.) in a company, before being reduced by accumulated depreciation.
Effect of Special Items on Income Taxes.
Deferred costs when reported other than as a part of tangible or intangible assets
represents land and improvements on land at acquisition cost. Land is not depreciated, but land improvements are normally depreciated over time.

Income Available to Common Stocks Incl. Extraordinary Items represents the bottom-line net income available to common stockholders, including the effects of extraordinary items - adjusted by minority interest, equity in affiliates, the U.S. GAAP adjustment, preferred distributions and all other adjustments to net income.
represents capital contributed by shareholders in excess of par value of common stock in return for shares issued to them.

represents cash inflow from the issuance of common stock. It also includes cash inflow from the sale of treasury shares. However, issuance of additional shares from a stock split is not included in Sale/Issuance of Common

Net income after taxes, adjusted by minority interest, equity in affiliates, the U.S. GAAP adjustment and extraordinary items, before preferred distributions and other adjustments to net income classified as Income Incl Extra Before Distributions

Construction in Progress - Gross represents the cost of construction in progress of fixed assets for a company's own use. The cost of construction in progress is normally not depreciated until the fixed assets are completed and placed in production.
common cash dividends paid
Other Properties/Plant/Equipment - Gross
Cash Receipts
other receivables.
Treasury Stock, when reported in the financing section of the cash flow statement, is henceforth classified as Sale/Issuance of Common or Repurchase/Retirement Common
total net receivables.

represents the value of common stock owned by the issuing company or its consolidated subsidiaries. Treasury stock is recorded at purchase cost, which is inclusive of par value, additional paid-in capital and retained earnings.
Current Tax - Total reflects the sum of Current Tax - Domestic, Current Tax - Foreign, Current Tax - Local, Current Tax - Other, and Current Tax - Total.

Represents deferred tax assets with a current maturity. When there is a timing difference between financial reporting and tax preparation practices, a company might have credits or additional tax liabilities from the government. Such credits or additional tax liabilities may be reported on a net basis, but if not, government credits may be reported as an asset and additional liabilities may be reported as a liability. The current portion of such assets is generally reported in the current assets section and classified as Deferred Income Tax - Current Asset [ADTC].
Deferred Tax - Total
gross property, plant & equipment.
Plan Assets - Domestic
Research and development costs expensed directly in the income statement.
Research and development costs expensed directly in the income statement classified as Research & Development Expense, Supplemental
Defined Contribution Expense - Domestic
Interest Cost - Domestic
Service Cost - Domestic
Discount Rate - Domestic
Depreciation/Depletion represents the sum of Depreciation and Depletion.
represents residual earnings from operations, not distributed to shareholders. It may represent accumulated deficit when a company incurs losses over time.
gross dividends on common stock.
Accrued Liabilities - Domestic
Current Port. of LT Debt/Capital Leases.
Total Debt represents total interest-bearing debt outstanding.
Total Long Term Debt, Supplemental reflects the sum of Long Term Debt Maturing within 1 through 10 Years and Long Term Debt - Remaining Maturities.
Pension Obligation - Domestic
Cash Payments
Funded Status - Domestic
Expected Return on Plan Assets - Domestic
Discount Rate - Domestic
Expected Rate of Return - Domestic
If debt issuances and reductions are not delineated separately, the total is classified as Issuance (Retirement) of Debt, Net
Deferred Tax - Domestic
Represents interest-bearing debt with maturities beyond 1 year. Long-term debt may consist of long-term bank borrowings, bonds, convertible bonds, etc.
Net Debt Including Preferred Stock & Minority Interest.
Notes payable/short-term debt.
represents interest-bearing debt with maturities beyond one year. Long-term debt may consist of long-term bank borrowings, bonds, convertible bonds, etc.
cash & equivalents.
Net Cash - Ending Balance represents the sum of Net Change in Cash and Net Cash - Beginning Balance.
Foreign Exchange Effects represents the increase/decrease in cash and cash equivalents due to changes in exchange rates.
Acquisition costs/expenses.

is applicable only if Net Income/Starting Line [ONET] is income after gains/losses from unusual activities. Because the operating section of the cash flow pursues changes in cash flow from operating activities, any unusual gain/loss included in net income computation needs to be reversed.
Unusual Expense (Income)
total equity.
Depreciation of investment properties disclosed outside of the cost of goods sold, for companies reporting in the IFRS or in other standards that treat these as investments
Other Operating Expenses, Total.

represents total expenses that are operating in nature, including variable costs directly related to the volume of sales, indirect operating costs, depreciation or amortization, operating provisions and other expenses incurred from operating activities.
Total Plan Expected Return
Total Pension Expense
Other Revenue, Total.
represents any residual value of shareholders' equity that belongs to shareholders.
Other Equity, Total.

Represents the bottom-line earnings per share available to common stockholders, excluding the effects of extraordinary items - adjusted by minority interest, equity in affiliates, the U.S. GAAP adjustment, preferred distributions, and all other adjustments to earnings per share.

Diluted EPS Excluding Extraordinary Items represents the bottom-line earnings per share available to common stockholders, excluding extraordinary items, adjusted by the effects of dilution. This value is adjusted by minority interest, equity in affiliates, the U.S. GAAP adjustment, preferred distributions and all other adjustments to earnings per share.
other financing cash flow
Financing cash flow items represent the sum of increase/decrease in deposits, increase/decrease in federal funds sold or REPOs and FHLB plus other financing cash flow.
Cash used for discontinued operations classified as Other Investing Cash Flow
Other Investing Cash Flow Items, Total represents all items reported within the investing activities in the cash flow statement, other than capital expenditures.
represents the impact of other operating cash flow items that cannot be classified as an adjustment item to net income, or an asset/liability as part of changes in non-cash working capital.
Changes in Working Capital
cash from financing activities that is the sum of financing cash flow items, cash dividends paid, issuance of stock, net and issuance of debt, net.
Actuarial Gains and Losses - Domestic
represents inventories of unsold finished goods on hand. These goods may have been produced internally or purchased for resale.
Represents the gross purchase value of assets that lack physical existence, before being reduced by accumulated impairment and intangible amortization.
gross accounts receivable from sales of trading assets such as inventories or from services provided.

Cash represents cash on hand, balances on demand deposits, and near-term savings and time deposits. These should be readily available for use and must be free from any contractual restrictions that limit their use in satisfying debts and other operational cash needs.

represents the bottom-line earnings per share available to common stockholders, including the effects of extraordinary items - adjusted by minority interest, equity in affiliates, the U.S. GAAP adjustment, preferred distributions and all other adjustments to earnings per share.
Diluted EPS Including Extraordinary Items represents the bottom-line earnings per share available to common stockholders, including extraordinary items, adjusted by the effects of dilution.
Other non-cash items.
non-cash items.
represents cash inflow on the issuance of long-term debt instruments in a company. Long-term debt may include long-term bank borrowings, corporate bonds, convertible bonds, etc.
Total Debt Issued represents cash inflow on changes in the level of debt of a company.
Cash from Investing Activities.
amortization of intangible assets reported outside the cost of goods and services sold
Amortization
Total adjustments to net income represents the sum of preferred dividend, general partners' distributions, miscellaneous earnings adjustments, pro forma adjustment, and interest adjustments.
Diluted Net Income represents the bottom-line net income available to common shareholders, including extraordinary items, adjusted by the effects of dilution.
Operating Income.
Total Plan Interest Cost

Represents long-term investment in securities (loans, bonds, and equity) of affiliate companies, where the affiliation may be defined as associated companies, joint ventures, and unconsolidated subsidiaries.
cash and short-term investments.
Total Special Items represents the sum of all non-recurring/unusual/extraordinary/one-off/special gains and losses that impact a company in any given period.
integer index of the item description field. You can use this index to find out what the value is from itemIx.
Represents long-term investments with maturities of 1 year or longer.
Normalized Income Before Taxes represents net income before taxes, excluding the effects of all non-recurring/unusual/one-off/extraordinary items.
Short-Term Investments
Total Current Assets less Inventory represents Total Current Assets minus Total Inventory.
net goodwill.
Goodwill - Net

Dilution Adjustment represents the adding back to reported net income the interest expense of debentures when assumed converted, and the adding back to the reported net income the convertible preferred dividends when assumed converted.
Quick Ratio represents Total Current Assets less Inventory divided by Total Current Liabilities.
Pension Benefits - Underfunded
other current liabilities.
Other Current Liabilities, Total.

represents current liabilities for industrial and utility companies. Current liabilities are liabilities that are incurred from operating activities and expected to be due within one year or an operating cycle of the company.
Total Capital Leases, Supplemental
Deferred Revenue - Long Term
sum of total liabilities and total equity.
Unrealized Gain (Loss) represents all unrealized/revaluation gains (losses) reported in the equity section by a company.
total liabilities.
represents the sum of Total Equity and Equity Non-Controlling Interests.
Total Operating Leases, Supplemental reflects the sum of Operating Leases Maturing within 1 through 10 years, Operating Leases - Remaining Maturities and Operating Leases - Interest Cost.
Full-Time Employees
total inventory.
represents changes in inventories during the period. An increase in inventories has a negative impact on cash flow from operating activities, and vice versa.
Long-term obligations from right-of-use contracts related to operating leases.
Other Liabilities, Total
other comprehensive income.
Net Profit Margin represents Income Available to Common Excluding Extraordinary Items divided by Total Revenue.
annual inventories - raw material.
Pretax Margin
If long-term debt issuances and reductions are not delineated separately, the total is classified as Long-Term Debt, Net
If short-term debt issuances and reductions are not delineated separately, the total is classified as Short Term Debt, Net
represents interest expense on debt of non-financial service companies, including insurance companies.
Interest Expense, Net Non-Operating represents Interest Expense, Non-Operating, reduced by Interest Capitalized, Non-Operating, and is usually a negative value.
interest income from operating section.
represents non-operating interest income, which is generated from non-operating investment securities such as certificates of deposit or bonds for non-financial service companies.
Interest/Investment Income, Non-Operating
Count of unique IDs of industry participants. Industry stands for an aggregate view of all  equity clearance activity for the date, symbol, and transaction type in question.

Represents accumulated interest for minority shareholders in subsidiaries that are less than 100 percent owned by the reporting parent company. The net worth of a subsidiary is assumed to be proportionally owned by the parent company and other minority shareholders according to their respective ownership percentage.

Minority Interest, Supplemental represents the share of earnings/losses in subsidiaries that belongs to shareholders other than the parent company when the parent company owns less than 100%, but more than 50%, of a subsidiary.
Interest Expense (Income), Net-Non-Operating, Total
interest expense from net operating income.
Interest expense that is reduced by interest income.
Net - Net Operating, Total
net property, plant and equipment.
Other Property/Plant/Equipment - Net
If the sale, issuance or repurchase of common stock is not separately delineated, then the total is classified as Common Stock, Net
Income Taxes Excluding Impact of Special Items represents total income taxes, adjusted by the tax impact of adding back non-recurring items, used in the computation of normalized.

represents changes in investments in securities and other investments. Investment, Net is utilized when a company does not delineate the sale of investments and the purchase of investments on the cash flow statement.
represents the number of primary common shares equivalent outstanding.
Expected Rate of Return - Domestic
Interest Expense, Net - Operating
Interest/Investment Income - Operating
Includes diverse cash flows that are reported outside of the operating, investing, and financing sections of the cash flow statement.
Total Plan Obligations
Interest Capitalized, Supplemental
maps each rep identifier to an instrument on a given day.
Tangible Book Value per Share, Common Equity.
Total Cash Dividends Paid.
aggregate of accumulated depreciation for each fixed asset.
Depreciation of fixed assets reported outside of the cost of goods and services sold.
depreciation.
Depreciation
cash interest paid represents interest paid in cash during the period Cash Interest.
Acquisition of investment securities of a company (rather than the whole business unit) classified as Purchase of Investments
Domestic Pension Plan Expense
Gross Margin represents gross profit divided by revenue. Gross margin is not available if either gross profit or revenue is missing or if revenue is negative.
Operating Margin.
Gross Profit represents a measure of a company's operating performance. Gross Profit states the profits earned from a company's revenues and direct costs.
cash taxes paid represents taxes paid in cash, net of tax refunds received, as reported by the company.
inventories that are WIP or work-in-progress.
Rental Expense, Supplemental represents the total value of rental expenses paid for offices, factories, machinery and equipment.
Income Taxes Payable represents income taxes payable to the government, where such tax liability is already confirmed in terms of amount and payable date.
represents cash outflow for acquiring new businesses. Reporting the cash flow for acquiring new businesses is not consistent among companies.
Franchise acquired (classified as Purchase/Acquisition of Intangibles).
Labor costs reported outside of the cost of goods sold, or not clearly labeled as part of the cost of goods sold, for businesses other than manufacturing and mining companies.
Total labor costs: Labor & Related Expense, Supplemental
Audit-Related Fees
accounts receivable value.
represents changes in accounts receivable during the period. An increase in accounts receivable has a negative impact on cash flow from operating activities, and vice versa.
represents cash outflow on the repayment of long-term debt in a company. Long-term debt obligations may be repaid upon maturity or replaced with new debt.
Total Debt Reduction represents cash outflow on changes in the level of debt in a company.
maps each rep to a ticker on a given day.
total revenue represents all items clearly presented as revenues, before the cost of goods sold and the gross profit.
Minority Interest - Non-Redeemable
Cost of revenue including labor costs reported within cost of goods sold & that are not clearly labeled as cost of goods sold.
cost of revenue represents total costs that can be directly attributed to the goods and services produced/purchased and sold.
Depreciation/amortization represents the sum of depreciation, amortization of intangibles, and amortization of acquisition costs.
Current Tax - Total
Deferred Tax - Total
Effective Tax Rate represents Provision for Income Taxes divided by Income Before Tax.
Represents long-term notes receivable, excluding the current portion of the long-term receivables. It also represents all other long-term receivables reported in the Non-current Assets section.
Short-Term Investments
Current Ratio represents Total Current Assets divided by Total Current Liabilities.
represents the weighted average common shares outstanding less the dilution of stock options for a given period. These shares are used to calculate Basic EPS.
Impairment/write-down/write-off/revaluation of investment securities arising from an unusual event.
Non-operational fixed assets that are long term.
Accrued interest income for companies other than financial service companies

Basic Normalized EPS represents the bottom-line earnings per share available to common stockholders, excluding the effects of all non-recurring/unusual/one-off/extraordinary items, after minority interest, equity in affiliates, the U.S. GAAP adjustment, preferred distributions and all other adjustments to earnings per share.
Diluted Normalized EPS
Common Stock represents the value of the most frequently issued class of stock; usually it provides a voting right, but is secondary to preferred stock in dividend and liquidation rights.
Total common stock represents the combined value of common stock and limited partner.
Deconsolidation of subsidiaries if ownership interest in a subsidiary is reduced classified as Sale/Maturity of Investment
Number of Common Shareholders
Total Plan Service Cost
Net Assets Recognized on Balance Sheet
Total Funded Status
If the sale, issuance, or repurchase of common and preferred stock is not separately delineated, then the total is classified as Issuance (Retirement) of Stock, Net.
net sales.
Diluted Weighted Average Shares represents the number of shares for Diluted EPS computation.
Net Income After Taxes
Normalized Income After Taxes.
represents a company's total revenues reduced by total expenses, before taxes on net income and all after-tax adjustments.
Represents income taxes estimated and expected to be paid within 1 year or an operating cycle, whichever is longer.
Represents the sum of Deferred Income Tax - Long-Term Liability and Deferred Investment Tax Credit
is the first line of a cash flow statement when a company employs the Indirect Method in the operating cash flow section.
Normalized Earnings before Interest and Taxes.
accumulated intangible amortization.
Amortization represents sum of amortization of intangibles, amortization of acquisition costs, amortization of deferred policy costs.
represents the net value of assets that lack physical existence, reduced by accumulated impairment and intangible amortization.
Intangibles - Net
Income from external grants received to cover research and development expenses.
represents any non-operating income or expense item that a company reports before income taxes
other net income.
Provision/allowances for inventory reported as a negative value classified as Inventories - Other
total assets of the company.
Income Tax - Total reflects the sum of Domestic Tax - Other, Foreign Tax - Other, Local Tax - Other, and Other Tax.
Excise taxes payment including Sales taxes, value-added tax (VAT) and other excise taxes reported within the cost of goods sold
Form of an unusual item related to the impairment of long-lived assets, certain identifiable intangibles, and goodwill related to those assets.
Audit Fees
Advances/deposits from customers for the products to be sold or services to be rendered classified as Customer Advances.
value.
Tangible Book Value, Common Equity.
advertising expense reported outside the cost of goods and services sold
Advertising Expense - Supplemental represents the total cost of advertising incurred by the company.

Revenue represents revenues from the sale of merchandise goods, manufactured products and services, and the distribution of regulated energy resources, depending on a specific company's industry. The item is used for industrial and utility companies.
investing income from operating section.

represents income or losses, other than interest, from investment securities. Such income may include dividend income, gain/loss on sale of investment securities, unrealized investment income or loss, equity earnings in affiliates, unconsolidated subsidiaries or joint ventures (before taxes), or any other net investment income.
Long-Term Investments represents the sum of LT Investments - Affiliate Companies and LT Investments - Other.
Provision for Income Taxes.

represents net income after taxes, adjusted by minority interest, equity in affiliates and the U.S. GAAP adjustment, before extraordinary items, preferred distributions and other adjustments to net income.
Represents operational expenses accrued, but not yet paid. As current liabilities, such expenses are expected to be paid within 1 year or 1 operating cycle, whichever is longer.
purchase of fixed assets.
capital expenditures.
interest expense.
Interest Expense, Supplemental.
prepaid expenses.
net goodwill.

Net income after taxes, adjusted by minority interest, equity in affiliates, the U.S. GAAP adjustment and extraordinary items, before preferred distributions and other adjustments to net income classified as Income Incl Extra Before Distributions

represents net income after taxes, adjusted by minority interest, equity in affiliates and the U.S. GAAP adjustment, before extraordinary items, preferred distributions and other adjustments to net income.
Provision for Income Taxes.
Income Taxes Payable represents income taxes payable to the government, where such tax liability is already confirmed in terms of amount and payable date.

The fiscal month corresponding to the interim reporting period.
The fiscal year corresponding to the interim reporting period.

total inventory.

total liabilities.

Represents current liabilities for industrial and utility companies. Current liabilities are liabilities that are incurred from operating activities and expected to be due within 1 year or an operating cycle of the company.

Other operating assets reported for the annual period.

represents the total amount of the current dividend requirement on issued preferred stock of a company (including its subsidiaries).
represents a company's total revenues reduced by total expenses, before taxes on net income and all after-tax adjustments.
Quasi-equity contracts reported for the annual period.
Quasi-equity liabilities reported for the annual period.

represents residual earnings from operations, not distributed to shareholders. It may represent accumulated deficit when a company incurs losses over time.
